text:
Zhaonan Wang
Zhaonan Wang is an Assistant Professor of Urban Studies and Computer Science at NYU Shanghai. His research interests lie in the interdisciplinary area of AI-urban science; he has published over 20 papers on top-tier AI/data mining conferences and journals. Zhaonan has just finished postdoc at University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, where he led the Geospatial AI team at CyberGIS Center. Zhaonan obtained his PhD in 2022 at Center for Spatial Information Science, the University of Tokyo, where he was awarded the Japanese Government (MEXT) scholarship. Zhaonan was also one of 37 nominees who were admitted to Peking University with College Entrance Exam exempted in Beijing 2010.
